Costa Rica Histórica

El Reventazón y la Línea a Limón

Postcard views of the Reventazón gorge and the San José–Limón rail corridor it threads, c. early 20th century Vistas postales del cañón del Reventazón y el corredor ferroviario San José–Limón, principios del siglo XX

The first two cards share the same printed banner — "Memorias de Costa Rica" — yet come from two different San José publishing houses. Read together, they show how a single river became a recurring subject for competing postcard series: one rendering the valley as scenic tableau, the other as a working transport corridor with the Atlantic Railway threaded along its bank. A third card extends the view along that same corridor — not the river itself, but the rail line that passes through its gorge en route from the capital to the coast. A fourth and fifth close the loop: the San José terminus where the journey began, and the Limón pier where it ended, ships and rail meeting at the Atlantic.

R-01

Valle del Reventazón with an unlabeled oxcart vignette

SeriesMemorias de Costa Rica, no. XV
PublisherAnto. Lehmann, Librería — San José de Costa Rica
FormatDual-image composite card, banana-leaf and bamboo decorative border

A two-panel composite typical of Lehmann's "Memorias" series. The left panel, captioned Valle del Reventazón, looks down into the forested gorge with a thin waterfall visible threading the far slope. The right panel is uncaptioned: a wooden house and a yoke of oxen drawing a cart, attended by several figures, framed inside a sketched bamboo border with banana leaves overhead.

"Valle del Reventazón" — printed caption, left panel
The right-hand oxcart vignette carries no place caption and may be a stock genre image reused across Lehmann's numbered series rather than a view specific to the Reventazón valley — a candidate for separate identification if a matching captioned card surfaces.
R-02

Reventazón River, via Limón Río Reventazón, vía Limón

SeriesMemorias de Costa Rica, no. 7
PublisherCéspedes — San José
FormatSingle-image card, English-language caption

A quieter, more documentary view than R-01: the river curves below a heavily forested slope, with the Atlantic Railway's track bed and a line pole visible hugging the near bank at lower right. The English caption — "Reventazon River, via Limon" — situates the card for a foreign or tourist market, framing the river as a waypoint on the line down to the Caribbean port rather than as scenery alone.

"Reventazon River, via Limon" — printed caption, lower right
Same series banner as R-01 — "Memorias de Costa Rica" — but a different publisher (Céspedes rather than Lehmann) and a different numbering sequence (no. 7 vs. no. XV), confirming the title was used generically across more than one San José publishing house.
R-03

Línea de San José á Limón three vignettes of the route, not the river

SeriesMemorias de Costa Rica, no. IV
PublisherAnto. Lehmann, Librería — San José de Costa Rica
FormatTriple-image composite card, sketched palm-frond divider, red printed title

Three working scenes from the San José–Limón line, joined by a drawn palm frond: a hillside plantation with a small steam locomotive rounding a curve at the foot of the slope; a busy loading siding where produce is being loaded onto open rail cars beside a thatched depot building, with a stationary engine and large flywheel at right; and a two-story plantation or line residence with deep verandas, set among palms.

"Línea de San José á Limón" — printed title, red ink
None of the three vignettes is captioned as the Reventazón gorge specifically, and the card is included here as a corridor companion to R-01 and R-02 rather than as a third Reventazón view: the same Lehmann "Memorias" banner, the same rail line, but plantation and depot scenes along the route rather than the river itself. Worth revisiting if a captioned Reventazón bridge or trestle card from this series surfaces later.
R-04

San José — Estación al Atlántico the journey's point of origin

PublisherEdición Tormo
FormatSingle-image card, printed title at top
SubjectTerminus building of the Atlantic Railway, San José

The terminus bookend to R-01 through R-03: this is the San José station from which the line to Limón departed, ornate in the mansard-roofed, cresting-and-fretwork civic style common to Costa Rican public buildings of the period. Several horse-drawn coches wait at the entrance — one numbered "72" on its door, suggesting licensed public hire carriages — alongside a small crowd of arriving or departing passengers and what appear to be newsboys working the steps.

"San José, C.R. — Estación al Atlántico (Edición Tormo)" — printed caption, top
Placed fourth rather than first so the sequence reads as the traveler would experience it in the catalogue's existing order — gorge, corridor, then back to San José — with R-05 now completing the loop at the far end of the line in Limón. A future reorder (San José terminus first, Limón terminus last, gorge and corridor between) would also be a reasonable editorial choice if the catalogue grows further.
R-05

Limón, Railway Pier the journey's far end

CaptionEnglish only — "Limón, Railway Pier C.R."
PublisherNot visible on this card
SubjectAtlantic Railway pier and harbor, Puerto Limón

The counterpart to R-04: where that card shows the line's origin in San José, this one shows its destination. A long pier carries the rail line out into Limón's harbor, lined with small trackside shelters, where at least two steamships are moored taking on cargo — almost certainly bananas, the dominant export of the line — alongside a sailing vessel further out in the bay. The foreground shows a railway passenger car and platform canopy, likely the Limón terminal station itself, looking out toward the pier and the ships beyond.

"Limón, Railway Pier C.R." — printed caption, top left
English-only caption (no Spanish equivalent printed on the card, unlike R-02), consistent with an export/shipping-trade or foreign-tourist market rather than a domestic one. With this card the catalogue now spans the full route: San José terminus (R-04) → the gorge (R-01, R-02) → corridor scenes (R-03) → Limón pier (R-05).

A note on publishers and series

R-01 and R-03 share a single publisher (Lehmann) but two different positions in his roman-numeral "Memorias de Costa Rica" run — XV and IV respectively — while R-02 carries the same banner under a different publisher, Céspedes, numbered 7 in arabic. R-04 stands apart from the "Memorias" banner entirely: an Edición Tormo card, included for its subject (the line's San José terminus) rather than any shared series identity. Until a wider sample is catalogued, these four should be read as four separate publisher runs unified by corridor and subject, not as one continuous series.

Costa Rica Histórica — archival postcard catalogue
